chunks.en: Before I take a day off, I usually write down everything that's still pending.
chunks.ja: 休みを取る前に、私はたいていまだ片付いていないことを全部書き出します。

chunks.en: I always tell one coworker who to contact if something urgent comes up.
chunks.ja: 何か急ぎのことが起きたら誰に連絡すればいいか、いつも同僚の一人に伝えておきます。

chunks.en: I try to finish or at least pause the urgent tasks before I leave.
chunks.ja: 急ぎのタスクは、出発する前に片付けるか、せめて止めておくようにしています。

chunks.en: When I come back, I check my messages first and slowly catch up.
chunks.ja: 戻ってきたら、まずメッセージを確認して、少しずつ追いつくようにします。

outline: Opening: how I prepare before time off
Point 1: write down pending work and name a contact
Point 2: finish or pause urgent tasks first
Wrap-up: catch up on messages when I return

modelTalk.text: Before I take a day off, I usually jot down everything that's still pending. It just helps me switch off, honestly. I'll write down what's half-finished and what someone might need to chase up. Then I tell one coworker who to contact if something urgent crops up. That way, nobody's left guessing while I'm gone. I also try to wrap up the time-sensitive stuff first, or at least pause it safely. If a task can't wait, I'd rather deal with it before I leave. The less I dump on my teammates, the better. When I get back, the first thing I do is check my messages and emails. I skim through everything and try to catch up bit by bit. It can feel overwhelming at first, but I take it slowly. Honestly, a proper handover makes coming back so much easier.

chunks.en: Before I take a day off, I usually write down everything that's still pending.
chunks.ja: 休みを取る前に、私はたいてい、まだやりかけの仕事を全部書き出しておきます。

chunks.en: I always tell one coworker who to contact if something urgent comes up while I'm out.
chunks.ja: 私が不在の間に急ぎの用件が出たら誰に連絡すればいいか、必ず同僚の一人に伝えておきます。

chunks.en: To be honest, I try to finish or at least pause the urgent tasks first.
chunks.ja: 正直なところ、急ぎのタスクは先に片付けるか、少なくとも一旦止めておくようにしています。

chunks.en: When I come back, I check my messages and catch up on whatever I missed.
chunks.ja: 戻ってきたら、メッセージを確認して、見逃していたことに追いつくようにしています。

outline: Opening: jot down pending work before a day off
Point 1: tell a coworker who to contact for anything urgent
Point 2: finish or pause the urgent tasks first
Wrap-up: check messages and catch up after returning

modelTalk.text: Before I take a day off, I usually jot down whatever's still hanging over me. To be honest, it helps me switch off properly once I'm gone. So I'll list the loose ends, and I'll flag anything that can't wait. I always tell one coworker who to reach out to if something crops up. That way, nobody's left scrambling, and they know exactly where to turn. Actually, I try to knock out the urgent stuff before I leave. If I can't finish it, I'll at least pause it somewhere sensible. That said, I don't stress about clearing every single thing. When I come back, the first thing I do is skim my messages. I'll skim through emails and catch up on whatever I missed. Sometimes it's a bit overwhelming, but I take it one step at a time. Handing things over well makes the whole break feel guilt-free, if you know what I mean.
